revision.text:
[[ja:赤井塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Akaizuka Kofun |image = File:Akaizuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |alternate_name = |group = {{ill|Mihata Kofun Cluster|en|Mihata Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11609095|lt=Mihata Kofun Cluster|1=Mihata Kofun Cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nabari, Mie|en|Nabari, Mie|qid=Q166183|lt=Nabari, Mie|1=Nabari, Mie}}, Kamiohata, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.50 |coordinates = 136|coordinates = 8|coordinates = 33.43 |iso = JP-24 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter: 22 m (estimated restored diameter: 30 m)
Height: 8.5 m |burial_facilities = Double-corridor {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = |associated_tombs = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Interred_individual = |mausoleum = |Historic Site = National Historic Site of Japan, part of {{ill|Mihata Kofun Cluster|en|Mihata Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11609095|lt=Mihata Kofun Cluster|1=Mihata Kofun Cluster}} |notable_features = |map = Japan Mie |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Akaizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Akaizuka Kofun''' (赤井塚古墳) is a {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Kamiohata, {{ill|Nabari, Mie|en|Nabari, Mie|qid=Q166183|lt=Nabari, Mie|1=Nabari, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mihata Kofun Cluster|en|Mihata Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11609095|lt=Mihata Kofun Cluster|1=Mihata Kofun Cluster}}, which is registered as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == Akaizuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed on a hill north of the Kobatagawa River, a tributary of the Nabari River, in the southern part of the Ueno Basin, western Mie Prefecture. The tumulus is located slightly south of the five keyhole-shaped tumuli of the Mihata Kofun Cluster on the northern plateau. The lower portion of the tumulus has been leveled due to agricultural activities, and no archaeological excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus is circular in type, measuring a current diameter of 22 meters (restored estimate: 30 meters) and a height of 8.5 meters{{Sfn|Nabari City Historical Archaeological Materials|2010|qq=}}. The burial facility is a double-corridor {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. Measuring 12.6 meters in total length, it is one of the largest chambers in the Iga region. The burial goods remain unidentified. The tumulus dates back to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}{{Sfn|Mie Prefecture Historical Archaeological Materials|2005|qq=}}. Within the Mihata Kofun Cluster, the five keyhole-shaped tumuli—ranging from the Denzuka Kofun of the late 4th century to the Kijin-zuka Kofun of the early 6th century—were constructed at intervals of one generation each, typeing a lineage of southern Iga chieftains. Akaizuka Kofun, as a large round tumulus built thereafter, marks the cessation of large tumulus construction and is significant for understanding the political transitions in the Iga region. The site was registered as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978 (as part of the Mihata Kofun Cluster). Entry into the stone chamber is currently restricted. == burial == [[File:Akaizuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] [[File:Akaizuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed plan of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] The burial facility is a double-corridor {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an entrance facing south. Its area are as follows{{Sfn|Mie Prefecture Historical Archaeological Materials|2005|qq=}}{{Sfn|Nabari City Historical Archaeological Materials|2010|qq=}}: * Total chamber length: 12.6 m * Main chamber: Length 5.7 m, width 2.4 m, current height 3.5 m * Corridor: Length 6.8 m, width 1.3 m, current height 1.4 m The stone chamber is constructed using gneiss and granite, with large stones measuring approximately 2 meters in size. The rear and front walls feature inward-leaning construction. The main chamber has a ceiling consisting of two large stone slabs{{Sfn|Mie Prefecture Historical Archaeological Materials|2005|qq=}}{{Sfn|Nabari City Historical Archaeological Materials|2010|qq=}}. Akaizuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Perspective view of the chamber Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Akaizuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) Akaizuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward entrance) Akaizuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward main chamber) Akaizuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == notes == {{Note list|qq=}} == Historical Archaeological Materials|2005|qq=}}|qq=}} ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Mihata Kofun Cluster|title=Nabari City Historical Archaeological Materials|publisher=Nabari City|isbn=|link=https://www.city.nabari.lg.jp/s061/060/070/050/01mihatakofungun.pdf|ref={{Harvid|Nabari City Historical Archaeological Materials|2010|qq=}}|qq=}} * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Mihata Kofun Cluster|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place names Dictionary|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Dictionary|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place names Dictionary|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}}, Volume 24: Mie Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|Mihata Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1983|qq=}}|qq=}} ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Hatsuomi Ōtsuka|authorlink=Hatsuomi Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=Mihata Kofun Cluster|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Mihata Kofun Cluster (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Akaizuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mihata Kofun Cluster|en|Mihata Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11609095|lt=Mihata Kofun Cluster|1=Mihata Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|赤井塚古墳|version=103805033|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:赤井塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Akaizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q123349012}}
